text:
There's an old, cozy bookstore filled with tall shelves lined with towering books. Every Saturday morning, a wise old owl named Olga hosts a read-aloud book club.

One chilly autumn day, a mysterious postcard arrives for Olga.

On the card, there's an illustrator bird whose design always changes slightly every week. On this card, it's a wise old owl. On the wing is a book.

The book? A pourquoi tale about a wise owl who could see into the dreams of animals.

Olga opens it to find advice: "When you lose your way in a forest, listen to the dreams of plants."

She begins reading. At night, she dreams of talking ferns.

A few weeks later, Olga spots ferns everywhere. They're whispering stories.

One night, Olga receives a letter from them—it advises her to listen to the winds. She finds they tell secrets.

One day, she wanders into a glade. There, she learns
